title: Tibbie Dunbar.
content:
Tune - "Johnny M'Gill."


I.

        O, Wilt thou go wi' me,
            Sweet Tibbie Dunbar?
        O, wilt thou go wi' me,
            Sweet Tibbie Dunbar?
        Wilt thou ride on a horse,
            Or be drawn in a car,
        Or walk by my side,
            O, sweet Tibbie Dunbar?

II.

        I care na thy daddie,
            His lands and his money,
        I care na thy kindred,
            Sae high and sae lordly:
        But say thou wilt hae me
            For better for waur,
        And come in thy coatie,
            Sweet Tibbie Dunbar!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Burns delivers a powerful performance in "Tibbie Dunbar."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
